title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt & David Thomas
description: A timeless guide for developers to sharpen their craft, avoid common pitfalls, and build better software through practical tips and real-world insights.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship that teaches developers how to write code that is readable, maintainable, and elegant.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: Stephen Hawking takes us on a journey through the cosmos, exploring black holes, the Big Bang, and the nature of time in an accessible masterpiece.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A mystical story of Santiago, a shepherd boy who dreams of finding treasure. A philosophical novel about following your dreams and listening to your heart.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: An exploration of the universe and humanity's place within it, blending science, philosophy, and history into a sweeping narrative of cosmic discovery.

title: You Don't Know JS
author: Kyle Simpson
description: A deep dive into JavaScript's core mechanisms, helping developers truly understand the language rather than just using it on the surface.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: Set in the American South during the 1930s, this Pulitzer Prize-winning novel confronts racial injustice and moral growth through the eyes of young Scout Finch.

title: The Gene: An Intimate History
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
description: A compelling history of genetics and its impact on humanity — from Mendel's peas to CRISPR — told with narrative brilliance by a Pulitzer-winning author.

author: George Orwell
description: A chilling dystopian novel about a totalitarian society under constant surveillance, exploring themes of power, freedom, and the manipulation of truth.

title: Design Patterns
author: Gang of Four
description: The foundational book on software design patterns, providing reusable solutions to commonly occurring problems in software architecture.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: Dawkins presents the gene-centered view of evolution, introducing concepts like the selfish gene and memes in this revolutionary and provocative work.

title: The Kite Runner
author: Khaled Hosseini
description: A story of friendship, betrayal, and redemption set against the backdrop of a turbulent Afghanistan — from the fall of the monarchy to Taliban rule.
